Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Notice Of Deposition,Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas And Compliance With Court Order;Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-15-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
B. Douglas Robbins for plaintiff and moving party: Clear Channel Outdoor, Inc.
Janet L. Everson for defendant and opposing party: Advertising Display Systems
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
As plaintiff previously conducted the deposition of Gerald Murphy, the Court will consider the motion as brought pursuant to CCP 2025.610(b). The motion is denied. The production of the billing records does not establish good cause to permit a subsequent deposition.

As the Court previously granted defendant's motion for a protective order regarding the deposition of Michele Carli, the Court will consider the motion as brought pursuant to CCP 1008. The motion is denied.

The motion to compel compliance with the deposition subpoena for Amanda Danaher is granted. The parties are ordered to schedule the deposition at a mutually convenient time. The deposition is to be completed by September 30, 2005. The Court makes no rulings as to the propriety of invoking either the attorney client or attorney work product privileges in responses to specific questions.

Plaintiff's motion for issue sanctions is denied. The motion for monetary sanctions is granted. The Court finds that defendant failed to comply with the previous order and made an evasive response to discovery. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2336.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Quashing The Taking Of Opposing Counsel'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-07-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
B. Douglas Robins for plaintiff and moving party: Clear Channel Outdoor, Inc.
Janet L. Everson for defendant and opposing party: Gerald Murphy, Jacobs, Spotswood, Casper & Murphy, LLP.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to quash deposition subpoena
Granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to the records only subpoena as to requests 5 - 7. Any and all responsive documents, consistent with the August 12, 2005 order relating to plaintiff's request for production of documents, shall be produced by September 12, 2005. The amount of the fee, identification of payment by case file name, and the general purpose of the work performed are relevant and not protected from disclosure by either the attorney client or attorney work product privilege. The motion is granted as to requests 1 - 4. The motion is granted as to the deposition of Timothy O'Leary. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
